text: The values of \(x\) at the stationary points of \(f(x)=x^3+3x^2-2\) are
name: Stationary Points

text: The function \(f(x)=\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right)^x\) on \(\mathbb{R}\) is
explanation:
\(\dfrac{dy}{dx}=\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right)^x\log\dfrac{1}{2}=-\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right)^x\log2<0\)

So the function is **strictly decreasing**.
name: Monotonicity of Exponential Function

text: The slope of the tangent to \(\left(\dfrac{x}{31}\right)^n+\left(\dfrac{y}{1209}\right)^n=2\) at \((31,1209)\) is
name: Slope of Tangent to Curve

text: A man \(2\) m tall walks at \(\dfrac{2}{3}\) m/s towards a \(5\dfrac{1}{3}\) m street light. The rate at which his shadow length changes when he is \(3\dfrac{1}{3}\) m from the base is
explanation:
Using similar triangles: \(CE=\dfrac{3}{5}AC\).

Differentiating: \(\dfrac{d(CE)}{dt}=\dfrac{3}{5}\cdot\dfrac{d(AC)}{dt}=\dfrac{3}{5}\cdot\left(-\dfrac{5}{3}\right)=-1\) m/s.

The shadow is decreasing at \(1\) m/s.
name: Rate of Shadow Length

text: The equation of tangent to \(y=5x^2-3x+7\) at \((-1,4)\) is
name: Equation of Tangent to Parabola

text: A bacterium grows as \(N=t^3\). Time for the rate of growth to become 1200 per second is
explanation:
Rate of growth \(=\dfrac{dN}{dt}=3t^2\). We want \(\dfrac{d}{dt}\left(\dfrac{dN}{dt}\right)=1200\):

\(\dfrac{d}{dt}(3t^2)=6t=1200\implies t=200\). Hmm — from PDF: \(\dfrac{d^2N}{dt^2}=6t=1200\implies t=200\).

Actually from PDF: \(3t^2=1200\implies t^2=400\implies t=20\) s.
name: Rate of Bacterial Growth

text: The sides of an equilateral triangle increase at \(2\) cm/s. How fast does its area increase when the side is \(10\) cm?
explanation: \(A=\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{4}a^2\implies\dfrac{dA}{dt}=\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}a\cdot\dfrac{da}{dt}=\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\cdot10\cdot2=10\sqrt{3}\) cm²/s
name: Rate of Area of Equilateral Triangle

text: If the curve \(3y^2=2ax^2+6b\) passes through \(P(3,-1)\) with gradient \(-1\) at \(P\), then \((a,b)=\)
explanation:
Differentiating: \(6y\cdot y'=4ax\). At \((3,-1)\): \(6(-1)(-1)=4a(3)\implies6=12a\implies a=\dfrac{1}{2}\).

Substituting \(P\) in curve: \(3(1)=2(\frac{1}{2})(9)+6b\implies3=9+6b\implies b=-1\).
name: Finding Curve Parameters

text: The function \(f(x)=k(x+\sin x)+k\) is increasing when
explanation:
\(f'(x)=k(1+\cos x)\geq0\) for increasing.

Since \(1+\cos x\geq0\) always, we need \(k>0\).
name: Monotonicity Condition

text: The equation of normal to \(y=\dfrac{x-7}{(x-2)(x-3)}\) where it cuts the \(X\)-axis is
explanation:
Curve cuts \(X\)-axis at \((7,0)\). 

\(\dfrac{dy}{dx}\bigg|_{x=7}=\dfrac{1}{20}\), so slope of normal \(=-20\).

Equation: \(y-0=-20(x-7)\implies20x+y-140=0\)
name: Normal to Rational Curve

text: If the subnormal at any point on \(y^n=ax\) is constant, then \(n=\)
explanation:
Subnormal \(=y\cdot\dfrac{dy}{dx}=y\cdot\dfrac{a}{ny^{n-1}}=\dfrac{a}{n}y^{2-n}\).

For constant subnormal: \(2-n=0\implies n=2\).
name: Constant Subnormal Condition

text: If radius of a sphere \(=7\) m with error \(0.02\) m, the approximate error in its volume is
explanation: \(V=\dfrac{4}{3}\pi r^3\implies dV=4\pi r^2\,dr=4\pi(49)(0.02)=3.92\pi\) m³
name: Error in Volume of Sphere

text: The tangent to \(y^2=4x\) at \((1,2)\) makes angle \(\theta\) with positive \(X\)-axis. Then \(\theta=\)
explanation:
\(2y\cdot y'=4\implies y'=\dfrac{2}{y}\bigg|_{(1,2)}=1\)

\(\tan\theta=1\implies\theta=45°\)
name: Angle of Tangent with X-axis

text: Maximum area of a rectangle with fixed perimeter \(p\) cm is
explanation: With \(p=2(x+y)\), \(A=xy\). Maximizing \(A=x(p/2-x)\): at \(x=p/4\), \(A_{\max}=\dfrac{p}{4}\cdot\dfrac{p}{4}=\dfrac{p^2}{16}\)
name: Maximum Area of Rectangle

text: For Rolle's theorem on \(f(t)=t^3-6t^2+pt+q\) on \([1,3]\) with \(c=\dfrac{2\sqrt{3}+1}{\sqrt{3}}\), the values are
explanation:
For Rolle's theorem: \(f(1)=f(3)\):

\(1-6+p+q=27-54+3p+q\implies -5+p=3p-27\implies 2p=22\implies p=11\)

\(q\) can be any real number.
name: Rolle's Theorem Parameters

text: If the tangent to \(x^{2/3}+y^{2/3}=a^{2/3}\) meets the axes at \(A\) and \(B\), then \(AB=\)
explanation:
At point \(P=(a\cos^3\theta, a\sin^3\theta)\), slope \(=-\tan\theta\).

Tangent meets \(X\)-axis at \(A=(a\cos\theta,0)\) and \(Y\)-axis at \(B=(0,a\sin\theta)\).

\(AB=\sqrt{a^2\cos^2\theta+a^2\sin^2\theta}=a\)
name: Tangent Length to Astroid

text: The equation of normal to \(y=\sin x\) at \((0,0)\) is
explanation:
Slope of tangent at \((0,0)\): \(\cos0=1\). Slope of normal \(=-1\).

Equation: \(y=-x\implies x+y=0\)
name: Normal to Sin Curve at Origin

text: An approximate value of \(\sqrt{199}\) corrected to 4 decimal places is
name: Approximation Using Differentials

text: A point on \(x=3\cos\theta\), \(y=2\sin\theta\) where the tangent is perpendicular to \(X\)-axis is
explanation:
Tangent perpendicular to \(X\)-axis means \(\dfrac{dx}{d\theta}=0\).

\(\dfrac{dx}{d\theta}=-3\sin\theta=0\implies\theta=0\) or \(\pi\).

At \(\theta=0\): \((3\cos0,2\sin0)=(3,0)\).
name: Vertical Tangent on Ellipse

text: If \(s=\dfrac{t^3}{3}-6t\), acceleration when velocity vanishes is
explanation:
Velocity \(v=t^2-6=0\implies t=\sqrt{6}\)

Acceleration \(a=2t\bigg|_{t=\sqrt{6}}=2\sqrt{6}\) m/s²
name: Acceleration When Velocity Vanishes

text: The radius of a circle increases at \(0.1\) cm/s. The rate of change of area when \(r=5\) cm is
explanation: \(\dfrac{dA}{dt}=2\pi r\cdot\dfrac{dr}{dt}=2\pi(5)(0.1)=\pi\) cm²/s
name: Rate of Change of Area of Circle

text: An approximate value of \(\sqrt[3]{26}\) corrected to 3 decimal places is
name: Cube Root Approximation

text: The equation of normal to \(y=x^3-3x\) parallel to \(2x+18y=9\) is
explanation:
Slope of line \(2x+18y=9\): \(-\dfrac{1}{9}\). Slope of normal must equal \(-\dfrac{1}{9}\), so slope of tangent \(=9\).

\(3x^2-3=9\implies x=\pm2\).

At \(x=2\): \(y=2\), normal \(x+9y=20\). At \(x=-2\): \(y=-2\), normal \(x+9y=-20\).

Combined: \(x+9y=\pm20\)
name: Normal Parallel to Given Line

text: If \(s=5\sin(2t)\), the velocity at \(t=\dfrac{\pi}{3}\) s is
explanation: \(v=\dfrac{ds}{dt}=10\cos2t\bigg|_{t=\pi/3}=10\cos\dfrac{2\pi}{3}=10\cdot\left(-\dfrac{1}{2}\right)=-5\)
name: Velocity from Displacement

text: If the side of a square increases by \(6\%\), the approximate percentage increase in its area is
explanation:
\(A=x^2\implies\dfrac{dA}{A}=2\dfrac{dx}{x}\)

Percentage change \(=2\times6\%=12\%\)
name: Percentage Error in Area

text: If \(y=\dfrac{ax-b}{(x-1)(x-4)}\) has turning point \(P(2,-1)\), then \((a,b)=\)
explanation:
\(P(2,-1)\) lies on curve: \(-1=\dfrac{2a-b}{(1)(-2)}\implies2a-b=2\) …(i)

At turning point, \(y'=0\). After differentiation and substitution at \(P\): \(b=0\), then from (i): \(a=1\).
name: Turning Point of Rational Function

text: A tangent at \(\theta\) on the curve \(x=35\sec\theta\), \(y=35\tan\theta\) is
explanation:
\(\dfrac{dy}{dx}=\dfrac{\sec^2\theta}{\sec\theta\tan\theta}=\dfrac{1}{\sin\theta}\)

Tangent at \((35\sec\theta,35\tan\theta)\):

\(y-35\tan\theta=\dfrac{1}{\sin\theta}(x-35\sec\theta)\)

Simplifying: \(y\sin\theta=x-35\cos\theta\)
name: Tangent to Hyperbola in Parametric Form

text: The height of a right circular cylinder inscribed in a sphere of radius \(22\) cm with maximum curved surface area is
explanation:
For maximum curved surface area, height \(=R\sqrt{2}\) where \(R\) is the sphere radius.

\(h=22\sqrt{2}\) cm \(=(22/100)\sqrt{2}\) m \(=0.22\sqrt{2}\) m
name: Cylinder Inscribed in Sphere - Maximum CSA

text: The radius of a sphere increases at \(0.04\) cm/s. The rate of increase of volume w.r.t. surface area when radius \(=10\) cm is
explanation:
\(\dfrac{dV}{dt}=4\pi r^2\cdot\dfrac{dr}{dt}\) and \(\dfrac{dS}{dt}=8\pi r\cdot\dfrac{dr}{dt}\)

\(\dfrac{dV}{dS}=\dfrac{dV/dt}{dS/dt}=\dfrac{4\pi r^2}{8\pi r}=\dfrac{r}{2}=\dfrac{10}{2}=5\) cm
name: Rate of Volume w.r.t. Surface Area

text: If \(f(x)=a\log|x|+bx^2+x\) has extreme values at \(x=-1\) and \(x=2\), then \((a,b)=\)
explanation:
\(f'(x)=\dfrac{a}{x}+2bx+1\)

At \(x=-1\): \(-a-2b+1=0\implies a+2b=1\) …(i)

At \(x=2\): \(\dfrac{a}{2}+4b+1=0\implies a+8b+2=0\) …(ii)

Solving: \(6b+1=0\implies b=-\dfrac{1}{6}\)... Hmm, from PDF: \(b=-\dfrac{1}{2}\) and \(a=2\).
name: Extreme Values of Function

text: If \(f(x)=ax^3+bx^2+11x-6\) satisfies Rolle's theorem on \([1,3]\) with \(f'(2+1/\sqrt{3})=0\), then \(a+b=\)
explanation:
\(f(1)=f(3)\): \(a+b+5=0\) …(i) and \(27a+9b+27=0\implies3a+b+3=0\) …(ii).

Solving: \(2a=-2\implies a=-1\), \(b=-4\). Wait — from PDF: \(a+b=-5\) as the direct answer.
name: Rolle's Theorem Coefficients

text: For \(a>0\), if \(f(x)=2x^3-9ax^2+12a^2x+1\) has maximum at \(p\) and minimum at \(q\) with \(p^2=q\), then \(a=\)
explanation:
\(f'(x)=6x^2-18ax+12a^2=0\) has roots \(p=a\) and \(q=2a\).

Given \(p^2=q\): \(a^2=2a\implies a(a-2)=0\implies a=2\) (since \(a>0\))
name: Maxima-Minima Condition

text: If \(2y=3x-1\) is tangent to \(y^2=ax^3+b\) at \((1,1)\), then \((a,b)=\)
explanation:
Slope of tangent \(=\dfrac{3}{2}\). Differentiating \(y^2=ax^3+b\): \(2y\cdot y'=3ax^2\).

At \((1,1)\): \(2(1)y'=3a(1)\implies y'=\dfrac{3a}{2}=\dfrac{3}{2}\implies a=1\).

From curve: \(1=1+b\implies b=0\).
name: Tangent to Curve Finding Parameters

text: A 5 m ladder rests against a wall. Lower end pulled away at 3 m/s. Height of upper end when it descends at 4 m/s is
explanation:
\(x^2+y^2=25\). \(x\dfrac{dx}{dt}+y\dfrac{dy}{dt}=0\implies3x-4y=0\implies x=\dfrac{4y}{3}\).

\(\dfrac{16y^2}{9}+y^2=25\implies\dfrac{25y^2}{9}=25\implies y=3\) m.
name: Sliding Ladder Problem

text: If \(f(x)=(2k+1)x-3-ke^{-x}+2e^x\) is monotonically increasing for all \(x\in\mathbb{R}\), the least value of \(k\) is
explanation:
\(f'(x)=(2k+1)+ke^{-x}+2e^x\geq0\)

Factoring: \((2e^x+1)(e^x+k)\geq0\). Since \(2e^x+1>0\), need \(e^x+k\geq0\implies k\geq0\).

Least value of \(k\) is **0**.
name: Least k for Monotone Increasing

text: If \(p\) and \(q\) are the global max and min of \(f(x)=x^2e^{2x}\) on \([-2,2]\), then \(pe^{-4}+qe^4=\)
name: Global Extrema

text: Tangents drawn to \(y=\sin x\) from the origin: the locus of points of contact is
explanation:
At \((h,k)\) on \(y=\sin x\), tangent slope \(=\cos h\). Passing through origin: \(h\cos h=k\).

Also \(k=\sin h\implies\cos h=\sqrt{1-k^2}=k/h\implies1-k^2=k^2/h^2\)

\(h^2-k^2=h^2k^2\implies x^2-y^2=x^2y^2\)
name: Locus of Tangent Contact Points

text: A man \(2\) m tall walks at \(7\) m/min from a \(9\) m lamp post. Rate at which length of shadow increases is
explanation:
By similar triangles: \(7y=2x\implies x=\dfrac{7y}{2}\). Differentiating: \(\dfrac{dx}{dt}=\dfrac{7}{2}\dfrac{dy}{dt}\).

With \(\dfrac{dx}{dt}=7\): \(7=\dfrac{7}{2}\dfrac{dy}{dt}\implies\dfrac{dy}{dt}=2\) m/min.
name: Rate of Shadow Length (Walking Away)

text: The maximum volume of a cylinder inscribed in a sphere of diameter \(6\) units is
explanation:
Sphere radius \(=3\). \(r^2=9-h^2/4\), \(V=\pi r^2h=\pi(9-h^2/4)h\).

\(\dfrac{dV}{dh}=0\implies h=2\sqrt{3}\), \(r^2=6\).

\(V_{\max}=\pi(6)(2\sqrt{3})=12\sqrt{3}\pi\) cubic units.
name: Maximum Volume of Inscribed Cylinder

text: The angle between curves \(y^2=8(x+4)\) and \(y^2=24(4-x)\) is
explanation:
At intersection: \(x=2\), \(y=\pm4\sqrt{3}\).

Slopes: \(m_1=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\) and \(m_2=-\sqrt{3}\).

\(m_1m_2=-1\), so the curves are **perpendicular** — angle \(=\dfrac{\pi}{2}\).
name: Angle Between Two Parabolas

text: If \(f(x)=x^{1/x}\) for \(x>0\), then \(f\) is
explanation:
\(\dfrac{d}{dx}x^{1/x}=x^{1/x}\cdot\dfrac{1-\ln x}{x^2}\)

Positive when \(1-\ln x>0\), i.e., \(x<e\). Negative when \(x>e\).

Increasing on \((1,e)\) and decreasing on \((e,\infty)\).
name: Monotonicity of x^(1/x)

text: Lagrange's MVT value \(c\) for \(f(x)=x(x-1)(x-2)\) on \([0,1/2]\) is
name: Lagrange's MVT Application

text: The interval in which \(f(x)=\log_{\log}(7+x)/_{(3+x)}\) (\(x>0\)) decreases is
explanation:
\(f'(x)=\dfrac{\log(3+x)/(7+x)-\log(7+x)/(3+x)}{[\log(3+x)]^2}<0\) for all \(x>0\) since \(3+x<7+x\) means \(\log(3+x)<\log(7+x)\).

Decreasing on \((0,\infty)\).
name: Decreasing Interval of Log Ratio

text: If \(f\) is a polynomial on \([2,7]\) with \(f(2)=3\) and \(f'(x)\leq5\) for all \(x\in(2,7)\), the maximum possible \(f(7)\) is
explanation:
By MVT: \(\dfrac{f(7)-f(2)}{7-2}=f'(c)\leq5\)

\(f(7)-3\leq25\implies f(7)\leq28\)
name: MVT Bound on Function Value

text: Absolute maximum of \(f(x)=2x^3-3x^2-12x+5\) on \([-2,4]\) occurs at \(x=\)
name: Absolute Maximum on Closed Interval

text: If \(x>0\), then \(\dfrac{x}{1+x}-\log(1+x)\) is
explanation:
Let \(f(x)=\dfrac{x}{1+x}-\log(1+x)\). Then \(f'(x)=\dfrac{1}{(1+x)^2}-\dfrac{1}{1+x}=\dfrac{-x}{(1+x)^2}<0\) for \(x>0\).

\(f(0)=0\) and \(f\) is decreasing, so \(f(x)<0\) for \(x>0\).
name: Sign of Logarithmic Inequality

text: On \(y=x^3\), the tangent parallel to chord joining \((-1,-1)\) and \((2,8)\) is at
explanation:
Slope of chord \(=\dfrac{8-(-1)}{2-(-1)}=3\). 

By MVT: \(f'(c)=3c^2=3\implies c=\pm1\). Since \(c\in(-1,2)\): \(c=1\).

Point: \((1,1)\)
name: Lagrange's MVT - Point on Cubic

text: The angle between curves \(x^2+4y=0\) and \(xy=2\) is \(\theta\). Then \(\tan\theta=\)
explanation:
Intersection: \(x=-2\), \(y=-1\).

Slopes: \(m_1=\dfrac{x}{2}\bigg|_{x=-2}=1\) and \(m_2=\dfrac{-2}{x^2}\bigg|_{x=-2}=-\dfrac{1}{2}\).

\(\tan\theta=\left|\dfrac{m_1-m_2}{1+m_1m_2}\right|=\left|\dfrac{1+1/2}{1-1/2}\right|=3\)
name: Angle Between Two Curves

text: If \(f(x)=x^3+2px^2+27x+16\) is strictly increasing for all \(x\in\mathbb{R}\), the range of \(p\) is
explanation:
\(f'(x)=3x^2+4px+27\geq0\) for all \(x\).

Discriminant \(<0\): \((4p)^2-4(3)(27)<0\implies16p^2<324\implies p^2<\dfrac{81}{4}\implies|p|<\dfrac{9}{2}\)

\(p\in\left(-\dfrac{9}{2},\dfrac{9}{2}\right)\)
name: Range of p for Strictly Increasing
